hook.text: An app asks to access your Google photos. You click Allow without giving it your Google password. That's OAuth.

buildup.text: OAuth lets you grant limited access to your data on one service to another service, without sharing credentials.

discovery.text: The flow issues a token with specific scopes: 'read photos' but not 'delete account.' Fine-grained control.

twist.text: OAuth handles authorization, not authentication. OpenID Connect adds identity on top — proving who you are.

climax.text: Every 'Sign in with Google' or 'Login with GitHub' button uses OAuth or OpenID Connect behind the scenes.

punchline.text: OAuth shares access, not passwords. Trust with limits.

quiz.question: What does OAuth handle?
options: Authorization — granting limited access without sharing passwords
